briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Climate Crisis Phenomena and Warnings
sections.content: Today, severe climate crisis phenomena and warnings due to climate change have been reported both domestically and internationally. Domestically, summers have become 4 weeks longer, and the number of heatwave days has surged [5]. In Busan, signs of prolonged algal blooms have led to strengthened criteria for issuing algal bloom warnings [4, 8, 14, 18, 22, 27]. Warnings have also emerged that global warming is progressing 5,000 times faster than the evolutionary speed of rice, potentially realizing concerns about food shortages [6, 23]. Internationally, climate change is threatening iconic seabird species with extinction [146, 149, 152, 153, 156, 160], and in Türkiye, concerns about reduced crop yields due to the absence of bees are growing, indicating an increasing threat to ecosystems overall [155]. Amidst these crises, gaps in perception still exist, such as former President Donald Trump arbitrarily interpreting the worst-case climate change scenarios [2, 150], and young Americans expressing concern about climate change but remaining skeptical about taking action [143].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Response and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Amidst these crises, diverse efforts continue domestically to address climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. The necessity of mainstreaming climate change response was raised in the Incheon region [1], and the Net Zero 2050 Climate Foundation and MultiLabSqueta have initiated cooperation in the carbon credit sector [3, 17]. Jeongeup City is dedicating full efforts to realizing a 'Sustainable Carbon-Neutral and Climate-Safe City' [20, 39, 41, 71], while Gyeongsangbuk-do and Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province have exceeded their greenhouse gas reduction targets [32, 58, 59, 63, 67, 70]. Furthermore, the Gyeongbuk Office of Education is also making efforts in the education sector, hosting the '2026 Carbon Neutrality Practice Contest' and operating professional development training for teachers [31, 44, 47, 54, 61, 62, 69, 74]. Solar power facilities have also been completed at the National Assembly sports field, enabling the National Assembly daycare center to operate on 100% renewable energy [45, 57, 117].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Policy Trends
sections.content: South Korea's energy transition policy is accelerating with a concrete roadmap. The government has announced the 1st Renewable Energy Master Plan, aiming to supply 100 GW of renewable energy by 2030 [83, 88, 89, 92, 101, 107, 116, 118, 119], and targets a 30% share of power generation by 2035 [92, 107]. This plan includes installing solar panels on the balconies of 2 million households and the roofs of new factories, with regional blueprints also presented, such as 37 GW for Jeonnam and 26 GW for Gyeongbuk [83]. Nonghyup Economic Holdings is holding a new and renewable energy general meeting to seek revitalization in rural areas [85, 93, 96, 97, 106, 111, 113, 127]. In Jeju Island, a 'direct PPA' method, which directly supplies electricity generated by wind power to hydrogen production, is set to be implemented for the first time in Korea [126, 139]. Furthermore, with the ongoing high oil price situation, market movements towards energy transition are also evident, as electric vehicle sales increased by 139% last month compared to the same month last year [140].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Awareness and Calls for Action
sections.content: The international community recognizes climate change as a public health crisis and points to a lack of preparedness for it. A WHO expert stated that declaring climate change a global public health emergency would be a turning point [142, 147], and in Wales, failing to prepare for a climate change emergency was criticized as 'negligence' [141, 167]. The UK Climate Change Committee emphasized that the UK was built for a climate that no longer exists, predicting temperatures to exceed 40°C by 2050, and stressed that investing in urgent adaptation measures would cost less than the damage from climate change [168, 169, 170]. Furthermore, warnings have been raised that climate change exacerbates poverty and deepens the global vulnerability crisis [164], and international discussions on response are actively taking place, with world leaders discussing climate change at international summits [161].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights the severity of the climate crisis, alongside the diverse domestic and international efforts simultaneously underway to overcome it. Sustained attention and action will become even more crucial for building climate resilience and achieving a carbon-neutral society. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: The first renewable energy master plan has been announced in South Korea, including the installation of solar panels on the balconies of 2 million households and the roofs of new factories. articles.description: The Korea Forest Service announced on the 20th that it will expand the creation of melliferous forests to protect bees disappearing due to the climate crisis. Over the past five years, the Korea Forest Service has been promoting the creation of melliferous forests with an annual target of 3,000 hectares. The total area of melliferous forests created so far is 17,818 hectares, equivalent to 24,955 soccer fields.

articles.title: Bypassing the Electricity Market…Jeju Produces Green Hydrogen with Wind Power Electricity via 'Direct PPA'
articles.original_title: 전력시장 안 거친다… 제주서 풍력 발전 전기 ‘직거래’로 그린수소 생산
articles.description: Korea's first 'Direct PPA' between the Haengwon Hydrogen Facility and coastal wind power will be introduced in June, bypassing the KEPCO grid for electricity cost savings and challenging for Korea's first 'Clean Hydrogen Certification'. This opens a path in Jeju to supply wind-generated electricity directly to hydrogen production facilities without market intervention, marking the first such instance of direct renewable energy supply for green hydrogen production in Korea.

summary.phenomenon: Korea's first 'Direct PPA (Power Purchase Agreement)' is scheduled to be introduced in June in Gujwa-eup, Jeju City, Jeju Province. This agreement will allow electricity generated by wind power to be supplied directly to hydrogen production facilities without passing through the electricity market, specifically between the Haengwon Hydrogen Facility and coastal wind power.
summary.cause: The aim is to reduce electricity costs by directly supplying renewable energy electricity to green hydrogen production facilities and to challenge for Korea's first clean hydrogen certification.
summary.outlook: By directly supplying renewable energy electricity without electricity market intervention, electricity cost savings are expected. This initiative is also anticipated to contribute to the expansion of green hydrogen production through the challenge for Korea's first clean hydrogen certification.
